seoTitle: How to Create a Paginated Report in Power BI Service (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to build a printable, multi-page paginated report directly in the Power BI Service browser without installing extra software.
intro: Turning a large data grid or financial ledger into a formatted, printable document usually meant opening a separate desktop tool. This guide shows how to build a paginated report directly inside Power BI Service's browser-based authoring engine, connecting it to an existing semantic model and exporting the result as PDF or Excel. It's aimed at analysts who need pixel-perfect, printable tables without extra software.

steps.heading: Start from a semantic model
steps.body: In your workspace, click the three-dot menu next to a published semantic model and select Create formatted table.

steps.heading: Add your data fields
steps.body: Check the boxes next to your target columns in the fields directory to drop them into the primary text layout.

steps.heading: Arrange your table structure
steps.body: Drag your selected fields up or down in the rows configuration drawer to set the display sequence, then switch to Page View to see pagination.

steps.heading: Adjust for print formatting
steps.body: Adjust column widths on the canvas so text wraps cleanly within standard margins.

steps.heading: Save and export
steps.body: Save your report with a descriptive name, then use the Export button to download it as PDF, Word, or Excel.

faq.question: Do I need Power BI Desktop to build a paginated report?
faq.answer: No, the browser-based authoring engine in Power BI Service lets you build paginated reports without opening separate desktop software.

faq.question: What formats can I export a paginated report to?
faq.answer: You can export to standard enterprise formats including PDF, Word, or Excel directly from the top ribbon menu.

faq.question: How does pagination work across long tables?
faq.answer: The rendering engine automatically handles cross-page typography mapping and splits your data cleanly across multiple pages.

faq.question: Can I connect a paginated report to an existing model?
faq.answer: Yes, you build it directly from a pre-published semantic model in your workspace rather than starting from a blank file.
